dry_bulk.confluence_note: A sharp divergence exists between record-breaking spot rates (BDI at 99.5th percentile) and persistent negative fundamentals characterized by weak Chinese steel demand.
dry_bulk.summary: Capesize and Panamax rates are currently in an expansionary regime with high momentum, despite China's iron ore portside inventories reaching all-time highs of 166mt. Subdued volatility and positive technical momentum are masking structural headwinds in the Chinese property and steel sectors.
dry_bulk.outlook: The rally appears technically driven and vulnerable to a correction if Chinese demand fails to clear record inventory levels.
dry_bulk.watch: China steel production figures and iron ore portside drawdown rates.

tanker.confluence_note: Quantitative and qualitative signals are perfectly aligned, with Clean Tanker Z-scores hitting extreme +3.28 levels alongside positive fundamentals.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is entering its second month of geopolitical disruption, with unnavigable straits forcing longer ton-mile alternatives and driving oil prices over $100. Clean tankers are exhibiting parabolic growth with a 150.1% 60-day rate of change as global inventories continue to draw. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2123.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2831.0 (ROC60 +78.3%).
key_signals: Global Crude Oil On Water YOY -1.6%
tanker.outlook: The structural shift in trade routes due to conflict suggests rates will remain elevated throughout the current quarter.
tanker.watch: Middle East geopolitical developments and OECD crude oil stock levels.

macro_note: The shipping landscape is dominated by a major geopolitical crisis in the Middle East, which has decoupled tanker performance from broader economic concerns. High oil prices now represent a dual threat: driving tanker demand through panic bidding while potentially cooling global dry bulk consumption.
